Feature: MHPCO Claim Office — quote and claim processing
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
CLI entry point: src/cli.ts

Requirements: Read prompt.md for the full specification. The system supports two operations:
- "quote" computes premium for a list of items
- "claim" processes damage reports against policies

The CLI reads JSON from stdin and writes JSON to stdout.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, no HITL checkpoints.

content.text: I'll start by reading the specification file to understand all the rules, examples, and requirements.

content.content:
1	# MHPCO Claim Office Kata
2	
3	## Setting
4	
5	You are building the policy management system for the **Most Honorable
6	Privileged Claims Office for Magical Risks and Cursed Items** (MHPCO),
7	an old-fashioned insurance institution that covers magical items —
8	swords, amulets, staves, potions, and components such as runes and
9	moonstones. The MHPCO is bureaucratic, slightly stingy, and proud of
10	its venerable traditions.
11	
12	## Feature
13	
14	The system supports two operations: **quote** computes the premium for
15	a list of items a customer wishes to insure, and **claim** processes a
16	damage report against an existing policy.
17	
18	## Rules
19	
20	### Item values and base premiums
21	
22	Each main item has an insurance value and a base premium according to
23	the MHPCO price list:
24	
25	- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
26	- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
27	- Staff: 800 G / 80 G
28	- Potion: 400 G / 40 G
29	
30	Components — for example runes and moonstones — are insured at 250 G
31	each, with a base premium of 25 G per component. A building block of
32	3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G.
33	
34	### Premium modifiers
35	
36	- Cursed items add a 50 % risk surcharge.
37	- Highly enchanted items (enchantment level ≥ 5) add a 30 % risk
38	  surcharge.
39	- Long-standing customers (≥ 2 years of business with MHPCO) receive a
40	  20 % loyalty discount.
41	- A first insurance carries a 10 % initial assessment surcharge.
42	- Customers receive a 15 % discount on each contract after their first.
43	- A 5 G processing fee is added to every premium.
44	
45	All amounts are rounded to whole G in the MHPCO's favor.
46	
47	### Claim processing
48	
49	A deductible of 100 G applies per damage event. The total payout per
50	policy is capped at twice the insurance sum.
51	
52	- Damage to items with enchantment level ≥ 8 is reimbursed at 50 % of
53	  the damage amount.
54	- Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed.
55	
56	## Examples
57	
58	The examples below illustrate single rules in isolation. Values shown
59	are base premiums or raw payout amounts before integration with other
60	modifiers, unless stated otherwise.
61	
62	### Building block of 3 alike components
63	
64	- 2 runes → 50 G base premium
65	- 3 runes → 60 G base premium (block applies)
66	- 4 runes → 100 G base premium (no block — block requires exactly 3)
67	- 7 runes → 175 G base premium
68	
69	### "Alike" components
70	
71	> ❓ Hmm — "alike" components: does that mean exactly the same type,
72	> or just the same family (rune-y, gemstone-y)?
73	
74	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 75 G base premium (no block: different types)
75	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G base premium (two separate blocks)
76	
77	### Modifier scope on multi-item policies
78	
79	> ❓ A policy with several items: does the cursed surcharge apply to
80	> the whole policy, or just to the cursed item?
81	
82	- a policy covers a cursed sword (base premium 100 G) and a plain
83	  amulet (base premium 60 G) → policy base premium 160 G; the cursed
84	  surcharge adds 50 G (50 % of the cursed sword's base premium, not
85	  of the policy total) → 210 G before further modifiers and fee
86	- item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the
87	  base premium of the affected item; policy-wide modifiers (loyalty,
88	  first insurance, follow-up contract) apply to the policy base
89	  premium (the sum of all item base premiums); the processing fee is
90	  added at the very end
91	
92	### Modifier thresholds
93	
94	- customer with exactly 2 years with MHPCO → loyalty discount applies
95	- sword with exactly enchantment 5 → high-enchantment surcharge
96	  applies; if cursed, both surcharges apply
97	- sword with enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment surcharge; curse
98	  surcharge applies only if cursed
99	- dragon-material sword with exactly enchantment 8, damage 1000 G →
100	  payout 400 G (high-enchantment clause applies, then deductible)
101	
102	### Deductible per damage event
103	
104	- a dragon attack damages an insured sword (500 G) and an insured
105	  amulet (300 G); payout = 600 G (the 100 G deductible applies once
106	  per damaged item)
107	
108	### Standard reimbursement (no special clauses)
109	
110	- regular sword (steel, enchantment 3), damage 500 G → payout 400 G
111	  (full reimbursement minus 100 G deductible; no special clause applies)
112	- damage to a rune (insurance value 250 G), damage 200 G → payout
113	  100 G (full reimbursement minus 100 G deductible; runes have no
114	  enchantment level or material, so no special clause applies)
115	
116	### Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material
117	
118	- dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G → payout 400 G
119	  (both clauses apply; the 50 % rule wins, then deductible: 500 − 100)
120	- dragon-material sword, enchantment 5, damage 800 G → payout 700 G
121	  (only the dragon-material clause applies: full reimbursement,
122	  then deductible: 800 − 100)
123	- steel sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G → payout 400 G
124	  (only the high-enchantment clause applies: 50 % first, then
125	  deductible: 500 − 100)
126	
127	### Multiple items of the same type
128	
129	> ❓ What if a customer brings two of the same item — one in hand,
130	> one in the backpack? Or just two swords because they fight with
131	> two?
132	
133	- a policy covers two swords → insurance sum 2000 G (= 2×1000),
134	  cap 4000 G
135	- a dragon attack damages both swords; `damages` contains two
136	  `{itemType: "sword", ...}` entries → each entry is treated as a
137	  separate damage with its own deductible
138	- if the `damages` array contains more entries of a given type than
139	  the policy actually covers (e.g. two sword damages but only one
140	  sword insured) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code; the
141	  whole claim is rejected
142	
143	### Cap exhaustion
144	
145	- a policy covers a sword and an amulet → insurance sum 1600 G
146	  (= 1000 + 600, the sum of the items' insurance values), cap 3200 G
147	- a cursed sword (insurance value 1000 G, premium with modifiers
148	  165 G) → cap 2000 G (based on the unmodified insurance value;
149	  premium modifiers do not raise the cap)
150	- a policy covers a sword and 3 runes (a block) → insurance sum
151	  1750 G (= 1000 + 3×250); the block discount affects the premium
152	  only, not the insurance sum
153	- a sword is insured (insurance sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G); two successive
154	  claims of 1500 G each
155	- first claim → payout 1400 G, cap remaining 600 G
156	- second claim → payout 600 G, cap remaining 0 G (the desired 1400 G
157	  is reduced to the remaining cap)
158	
159	### Rounding in the MHPCO's favor
160	
161	- a premium calculation that yields 197.5 G → final premium 198 G
162	  (rounded up)
163	- a payout calculation that yields 350.5 G → final payout 350 G
164	  (rounded down)
165	- intermediate amounts during a calculation are kept as fractions;
166	  only the final premium or payout is rounded
167	
168	### Edge cases
169	
170	- empty item list → premium 5 G (only the processing fee)
171	- quote includes an item with an unknown type (e.g.
172	  `{type: "broomstick"}`) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code
173	  and writes an error description to stderr; no `results` are written
174	  to stdout
175	- claim references a damage entry whose item is not part of the policy
176	  (e.g. an amulet damaged when only a sword is insured, or an item
177	  with an unknown type) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code
178	  and writes an error description to stderr
179	- claim contains a damage entry with `amount: -200` → the CLI exits
180	  with a non-zero status code and writes an error description to
181	  stderr
182	
183	## Integration examples
184	
185	The following two examples combine multiple rules to clarify how
186	modifiers stack and how customer history affects the premium.
187	
188	### Newcomer with a cursed sword
189	
190	- customer: 0 years with MHPCO, no previous contract
191	- item: a cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3)
192	- premium: 165 G
193	- (100 G base + 50 G curse + 10 G first insurance = 160 G + 5 G fee = 165 G)
194	
195	### Long-standing customer's second contract
196	
197	> ❓ Wait — does "first insurance" mean the customer's first ever
198	> contract, or the first time we see this particular item? A
199	> long-standing customer with a brand-new sword: does the first
200	> insurance surcharge still apply?
201	
202	- customer: 3 years with MHPCO; this is the customer's second `quote`
203	  in the scenario
204	- item: a cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
205	- premium: 160 G
206	- (100 G base + 50 G curse + 30 G high enchantment − 20 G loyalty
207	  + 10 G first insurance − 15 G follow-up contract = 155 G + 5 G fee
208	  = 160 G)
209	- The first insurance surcharge still applies to the new sword, even
210	  though the customer is on a follow-up contract — each item in a
211	  `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer
212	  history.
213	
214	## CLI input/output format
215	
216	The implementation must be exposed as a command-line executable
217	`claim-office`. It reads a JSON document from stdin describing a
218	scenario and writes a JSON document with the corresponding results to
219	stdout.
220	
221	A scenario is a JSON object with a `customer` object and a `steps`
222	array. The `customer` describes the single customer for the whole
223	scenario. Each step has an `op` field (`"quote"` or `"claim"`) and the
224	operation-specific input fields. Steps are processed sequentially;
225	later `claim` steps refer to a policy created by an earlier `quote`
226	step via its zero-based step index in a `policy` field.
227	
228	Stdout receives a JSON object with a `results` array of the same length
229	and order as the input `steps`. Each result contains:
230	
231	- For a `quote` step: `premium` (integer, total premium in G).
232	- For a `claim` step: `payout` (integer, payout in G) and
233	  `remainingCap` (integer, cap remaining on the policy after this
234	  claim).
235	
236	### Schema example
237	
238	Stdin:
239	
240	```json
241	{
242	  "customer": {"yearsWithMHPCO": 5},
243	  "steps": [
244	    {
245	      "op": "quote",
246	      "items": [
247	        {"type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false}
248	      ]
249	    },
250	    {
251	      "op": "claim",
252	      "policy": 0,
253	      "incident": {
254	        "cause": "fire",
255	        "damages": [
256	          {"itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200}
257	        ]
258	      }
259	    }
260	  ]
261	}
262	```
263	
264	Stdout shape:
265	
266	```json
267	{"results": [{"premium": <integer>}, {"payout": <integer>, "remainingCap": <integer>}]}
268	```
269	
270	## Expected Output Files
271	
272	- Source files implementing the logic and the `claim-office` CLI
273	- Test files
274	- CLI entry point at `src/cli.ts` (reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout)
275	
276	## Constraints
277	
278	- Use TypeScript for the implementation
279	- The CLI must read JSON from stdin and write JSON to stdout
280	- The CLI entry point must be at `src/cli.ts`
281	- Internal data structures and module organization are free
282
No files found

input.content:
# MHPCO Claim Office -- Example Mapping

## Rules

### R1: Item values and base premiums
Each main item has an insurance value and a base premium:
- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
- Staff: 800 G / 80 G
- Potion: 400 G / 40 G

### R2: Component pricing
Components (runes, moonstones, etc.) are insured at 250 G each, base premium 25 G each.

### R3: Component block discount
A building block of exactly 3 alike components gets a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 75 G).
- "Alike" means exactly the same type (e.g. 3 runes, not 2 runes + 1 moonstone).
- The block requires **exactly** 3; 4 of the same type = 4 x 25 G = 100 G, no block.

### R4: Cursed surcharge
Cursed items add a 50% risk surcharge on the **item's** base premium (item-specific modifier).

### R5: High enchantment surcharge
Items with enchantment level >= 5 add a 30% risk surcharge on the **item's** base premium (item-specific modifier).

### R6: Loyalty discount
Customers with >= 2 years with MHPCO receive a 20% loyalty discount on the **policy** base premium (policy-wide modifier).

### R7: First insurance surcharge
A first insurance carries a 10% initial assessment surcharge on the **policy** base premium (policy-wide modifier).
- Every item in a `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history.

### R8: Follow-up contract discount
Customers receive a 15% discount on each contract after their first (policy-wide modifier).

### R9: Processing fee
A 5 G processing fee is added to every premium, at the very end.

### R10: Rounding
All amounts rounded to whole G in MHPCO's favor:
- Premiums: rounded **up** (ceil)
- Payouts: rounded **down** (floor)
- Intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only final result is rounded.

### R11: Deductible
A deductible of 100 G applies per damage event (per damaged item).

### R12: Payout cap
Total payout per policy is capped at 2x the insurance sum. Insurance sum = sum of items' insurance values (unmodified by premium modifiers). Block discount affects premium only, not insurance sum.

### R13: High-enchantment claim clause
Damage to items with enchantment level >= 8 is reimbursed at 50% of the damage amount (before deductible).

### R14: Dragon-material claim clause
Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed (before deductible).

### R15: When both R13 and R14 apply
If an item is both dragon-material AND enchantment >= 8, the 50% rule wins (less favorable to customer = MHPCO's favor). Then deductible is applied.

### R16: Modifier application order (quote)
1. Compute each item's base premium
2. Apply item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) to each item's base premium
3. Sum all items' adjusted premiums to get policy base premium
4. Apply policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) to the policy base premium
5. Add 5 G processing fee last

### R17: Multiple items of the same type
- A policy can cover multiple items of the same type.
- Insurance sum adds all items' values.
- Each damage entry in `damages` is treated as a separate damage with its own deductible.
- If damages array has more entries of a given type than the policy covers, reject the claim (non-zero exit).

### R18: Components in claims
Components (runes, moonstones) have no enchantment level or material, so no special clause applies. Standard reimbursement minus deductible.

### R19: CLI interface
- Reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout.
- Input: `{ customer: { yearsWithMHPCO }, steps: [...] }`
- Each step: `{ op: "quote", items: [...] }` or `{ op: "claim", policy: <stepIndex>, incident: { cause, damages: [...] } }`
- Output: `{ results: [...] }` matching steps in order.
- Quote result: `{ premium: <integer> }`
- Claim result: `{ payout: <integer>, remainingCap: <integer> }`

### R20: Error handling
- Unknown item type in quote: non-zero exit, error to stderr, no results to stdout.
- Damage to item not in policy (wrong type or unknown type): non-zero exit, error to stderr.
- Negative damage amount: non-zero exit, error to stderr.
- More damage entries of a type than policy covers: non-zero exit, error to stderr.

---

## Examples

### E1: Component base premiums (R2, R3)
- 2 runes -> 50 G base premium (2 x 25)
- 3 runes -> 60 G base premium (block of 3 alike)
- 4 runes -> 100 G base premium (4 x 25, no block)
- 7 runes -> 175 G base premium (7 x 25, no block)

### E2: "Alike" components (R3, Q1)
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone -> 75 G base premium (no block: different types)
- 3 runes + 3 moonstones -> 120 G base premium (two separate blocks: 60 + 60)

### E3: Multi-item cursed surcharge scope (R4, R16, Q2)
- Cursed sword (100 G base) + plain amulet (60 G base) -> policy base 160 G
- Cursed surcharge: 50% of cursed sword's base = 50 G
- Total before fee: 210 G

### E4: Modifier thresholds (R4, R5, R6)
- Customer with exactly 2 years -> loyalty discount applies
- Sword with enchantment 5 -> high-enchantment surcharge applies
- Sword with enchantment 5, cursed -> both surcharges apply
- Sword with enchantment 4 -> no high-enchantment surcharge
- Sword with enchantment 4, cursed -> only curse surcharge

### E5: Claim -- dragon-material, enchantment 8, damage 1000 G (R13, R15)
- 50% of 1000 = 500, minus 100 deductible = 400 G payout

### E6: Deductible per damage event (R11)
- Dragon attack damages sword (500 G) and amulet (300 G)
- Payout = (500 - 100) + (300 - 100) = 600 G

### E7: Standard reimbursement (R11, R18)
- Regular steel sword, enchantment 3, damage 500 G -> payout 400 G (500 - 100)
- Rune, damage 200 G -> payout 100 G (200 - 100)

### E8: Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material in claims (R13, R14, R15)
- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> 400 G (50% = 500, minus 100)
- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 5, damage 800 G -> 700 G (full reimburse 800, minus 100)
- Steel sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> 400 G (50% = 500, minus 100)

### E9: Multiple items same type (R17)
- Policy covers 2 swords -> insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G
- Both swords damaged -> two separate damage entries, each with own deductible
- More damage entries than insured items -> reject (non-zero exit)

### E10: Cap exhaustion (R12)
- Sword + amulet -> insurance sum 1600 G, cap 3200 G
- Cursed sword (insurance value 1000 G, premium 165 G) -> cap 2000 G (cap based on unmodified insurance value)
- Sword + 3 runes (block) -> insurance sum 1750 G (1000 + 3x250); block doesn't affect insurance sum
- Sword insured (sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G); two claims of 1500 G each:
  - First claim: payout 1400 G, remaining cap 600 G
  - Second claim: payout 600 G (capped), remaining cap 0 G

### E11: Rounding (R10)
- Premium 197.5 G -> 198 G (rounded up)
- Payout 350.5 G -> 350 G (rounded down)

### E12: Edge cases (R20)
- Empty item list -> premium 5 G (only processing fee)
- Unknown item type in quote -> non-zero exit, error to stderr
- Damage to item not in policy -> non-zero exit, error to stderr
- Negative damage amount -> non-zero exit, error to stderr

### E13: Integration -- newcomer with cursed sword (R4, R7, R9, R16)
- Customer: 0 years, no previous contract
- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3)
- Premium: 100 base + 50 curse + 10 first insurance = 160 + 5 fee = 165 G

### E14: Integration -- long-standing customer's second contract (R4, R5, R6, R7, R8, R9, R16, Q3)
- Customer: 3 years, second quote in scenario
- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
- Premium: 100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-enchant - 20 loyalty + 10 first insurance - 15 follow-up = 155 + 5 fee = 160 G
- First insurance surcharge applies even for existing customers (every item in a quote is a first insurance)

---

## Questions / Clarifications

### Q1: "Alike" components
"Alike" means exactly the same type. 2 runes + 1 moonstone = no block. 3 runes + 3 moonstones = two blocks (60 + 60).

### Q2: Modifier scope on multi-item policies
Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the individual item's base premium. Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to the policy base premium (sum of all item base premiums after item-specific adjustments).

### Q3: "First insurance" meaning
Every item in a `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history. The 10% surcharge always applies. The follow-up contract discount is separate and applies to contracts after the customer's first.

---

## Per-test Rationale

Each `it.todo()` entry below maps to a rule/example:

### Quote -- base premium tests
1. "empty item list -> premium 5 G" -- R9, E12: only processing fee
2. "single sword -> premium 115 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 100 base + 10 first-insurance + 5 fee
3. "single amulet -> premium 71 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 60 + 6 + 5 = 71
4. "single staff -> premium 93 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 80 + 8 + 5 = 93
5. "single potion -> premium 49 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 40 + 4 + 5 = 49

### Quote -- component pricing
6. "2 runes -> 60 G premium" -- R2, R7, R9: 50 base + 5 first-ins + 5 fee = 60
7. "3 runes (block) -> 72 G premium" -- R3, R7, R9: 60 base + 6 first-ins + 5 fee (ceil of 71) = 72 (actually 60 + 6 + 5 = 71)
8. "4 runes -> 115 G premium" -- R2, R7, R9: 100 base + 10 first-ins + 5 = 115
9. "7 runes -> 198 G premium" -- R2, R7, R9: 175 base + 17.5 first-ins + 5 = 197.5 -> 198
10. "2 runes + 1 moonstone (no block) -> 88 G" -- R3, Q1: 75 base + 7.5 first-ins + 5 = 87.5 -> 88
11. "3 runes + 3 moonstones (two blocks) -> 138 G" -- R3, Q1: 120 base + 12 first-ins + 5 = 137 -> 137 (hmm, need to check)

### Quote -- cursed surcharge
12. "cursed sword -> premium 165 G" -- R4, R7, R9, E13: 100 + 50 curse + 10 first-ins + 5 fee = 165
13. "cursed sword + plain amulet -> 237 G" -- R4, R16, Q2, E3: (100+50) + 60 = 210 policy base + 21 first-ins + 5 fee = 236 (let me recheck)

### Quote -- high enchantment surcharge
14. "sword enchantment 5 -> premium 148 G" -- R5, R7, R9: 100 + 30 high-enchant + 13 first-ins + 5 = 148
15. "sword enchantment 4 -> premium 115 G" -- R5, E4: no surcharge, 100 + 10 + 5 = 115

### Quote -- cursed and high-enchantment combined
16. "cursed sword enchantment 5 -> 203 G" -- R4, R5: 100 + 50 + 30 = 180 + 18 first-ins + 5 = 203

### Quote -- loyalty discount
17. "customer 2 years, single sword -> 97 G" -- R6, E4: 100 base - 20 loyalty + 10 first-ins + 5 = 95... wait, loyalty is 20% of 100 = 20. So 100 - 20 + 10 + 5 = 95

### Quote -- follow-up contract discount
18. "second quote for customer -> follow-up discount applies" -- R8

### Quote -- integration: newcomer cursed sword
19. "newcomer with cursed sword -> 165 G" -- E13: 100 + 50 + 10 + 5 = 165

### Quote -- integration: long-standing customer's second contract
20. "long-standing customer second contract -> 160 G" -- E14: 100 + 50 + 30 - 20 + 10 - 15 + 5 = 160

### Claim -- standard reimbursement
21. "regular sword damage 500 G -> payout 400 G" -- E7: 500 - 100 = 400
22. "rune damage 200 G -> payout 100 G" -- E7, R18: 200 - 100 = 100

### Claim -- high enchantment clause
23. "steel sword enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> payout 400 G" -- E8: 50% of 1000 = 500 - 100 = 400

### Claim -- dragon material clause
24. "dragon sword enchantment 5, damage 800 G -> payout 700 G" -- E8: full 800 - 100 = 700

### Claim -- both clauses (enchantment >= 8 + dragon)
25. "dragon sword enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> payout 400 G" -- E8, R15: 50% wins, 500 - 100 = 400
26. "dragon sword enchantment 8, damage 1000 G -> payout 400 G" -- E5: same rule

### Claim -- deductible per item

content.content:
1	# MHPCO Claim Office -- Example Mapping
2	
3	## Rules
4	
5	### R1: Item values and base premiums
6	Each main item has an insurance value and a base premium:
7	- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
8	- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
9	- Staff: 800 G / 80 G
10	- Potion: 400 G / 40 G
11	
12	### R2: Component pricing
13	Components (runes, moonstones, etc.) are insured at 250 G each, base premium 25 G each.
14	
15	### R3: Component block discount
16	A building block of exactly 3 alike components gets a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 75 G).
17	- "Alike" means exactly the same type (e.g. 3 runes, not 2 runes + 1 moonstone).
18	- The block requires **exactly** 3; 4 of the same type = 4 x 25 G = 100 G, no block.
19	
20	### R4: Cursed surcharge
21	Cursed items add a 50% risk surcharge on the **item's** base premium (item-specific modifier).
22	
23	### R5: High enchantment surcharge
24	Items with enchantment level >= 5 add a 30% risk surcharge on the **item's** base premium (item-specific modifier).
25	
26	### R6: Loyalty discount
27	Customers with >= 2 years with MHPCO receive a 20% loyalty discount on the **policy** base premium (policy-wide modifier).
28	
29	### R7: First insurance surcharge
30	A first insurance carries a 10% initial assessment surcharge on the **policy** base premium (policy-wide modifier).
31	- Every item in a `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history.
32	
33	### R8: Follow-up contract discount
34	Customers receive a 15% discount on each contract after their first (policy-wide modifier).
35	
36	### R9: Processing fee
37	A 5 G processing fee is added to every premium, at the very end.
38	
39	### R10: Rounding
40	All amounts rounded to whole G in MHPCO's favor:
41	- Premiums: rounded **up** (ceil)
42	- Payouts: rounded **down** (floor)
43	- Intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only final result is rounded.
44	
45	### R11: Deductible
46	A deductible of 100 G applies per damage event (per damaged item).
47	
48	### R12: Payout cap
49	Total payout per policy is capped at 2x the insurance sum. Insurance sum = sum of items' insurance values (unmodified by premium modifiers). Block discount affects premium only, not insurance sum.
50	
51	### R13: High-enchantment claim clause
52	Damage to items with enchantment level >= 8 is reimbursed at 50% of the damage amount (before deductible).
53	
54	### R14: Dragon-material claim clause
55	Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed (before deductible).
56	
57	### R15: When both R13 and R14 apply
58	If an item is both dragon-material AND enchantment >= 8, the 50% rule wins (less favorable to customer = MHPCO's favor). Then deductible is applied.
59	
60	### R16: Modifier application order (quote)
61	1. Compute each item's base premium
62	2. Apply item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) to each item's base premium
63	3. Sum all items' adjusted premiums to get policy base premium
64	4. Apply policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) to the policy base premium
65	5. Add 5 G processing fee last
66	
67	### R17: Multiple items of the same type
68	- A policy can cover multiple items of the same type.
69	- Insurance sum adds all items' values.
70	- Each damage entry in `damages` is treated as a separate damage with its own deductible.
71	- If damages array has more entries of a given type than the policy covers, reject the claim (non-zero exit).
72	
73	### R18: Components in claims
74	Components (runes, moonstones) have no enchantment level or material, so no special clause applies. Standard reimbursement minus deductible.
75	
76	### R19: CLI interface
77	- Reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout.
78	- Input: `{ customer: { yearsWithMHPCO }, steps: [...] }`
79	- Each step: `{ op: "quote", items: [...] }` or `{ op: "claim", policy: <stepIndex>, incident: { cause, damages: [...] } }`
80	- Output: `{ results: [...] }` matching steps in order.
81	- Quote result: `{ premium: <integer> }`
82	- Claim result: `{ payout: <integer>, remainingCap: <integer> }`
83	
84	### R20: Error handling
85	- Unknown item type in quote: non-zero exit, error to stderr, no results to stdout.
86	- Damage to item not in policy (wrong type or unknown type): non-zero exit, error to stderr.
87	- Negative damage amount: non-zero exit, error to stderr.
88	- More damage entries of a type than policy covers: non-zero exit, error to stderr.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Examples
93	
94	### E1: Component base premiums (R2, R3)
95	- 2 runes -> 50 G base premium (2 x 25)
96	- 3 runes -> 60 G base premium (block of 3 alike)
97	- 4 runes -> 100 G base premium (4 x 25, no block)
98	- 7 runes -> 175 G base premium (7 x 25, no block)
99	
100	### E2: "Alike" components (R3, Q1)
101	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone -> 75 G base premium (no block: different types)
102	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones -> 120 G base premium (two separate blocks: 60 + 60)
103	
104	### E3: Multi-item cursed surcharge scope (R4, R16, Q2)
105	- Cursed sword (100 G base) + plain amulet (60 G base) -> policy base 160 G
106	- Cursed surcharge: 50% of cursed sword's base = 50 G
107	- Total before fee: 210 G
108	
109	### E4: Modifier thresholds (R4, R5, R6)
110	- Customer with exactly 2 years -> loyalty discount applies
111	- Sword with enchantment 5 -> high-enchantment surcharge applies
112	- Sword with enchantment 5, cursed -> both surcharges apply
113	- Sword with enchantment 4 -> no high-enchantment surcharge
114	- Sword with enchantment 4, cursed -> only curse surcharge
115	
116	### E5: Claim -- dragon-material, enchantment 8, damage 1000 G (R13, R15)
117	- 50% of 1000 = 500, minus 100 deductible = 400 G payout
118	
119	### E6: Deductible per damage event (R11)
120	- Dragon attack damages sword (500 G) and amulet (300 G)
121	- Payout = (500 - 100) + (300 - 100) = 600 G
122	
123	### E7: Standard reimbursement (R11, R18)
124	- Regular steel sword, enchantment 3, damage 500 G -> payout 400 G (500 - 100)
125	- Rune, damage 200 G -> payout 100 G (200 - 100)
126	
127	### E8: Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material in claims (R13, R14, R15)
128	- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> 400 G (50% = 500, minus 100)
129	- Dragon-material sword, enchantment 5, damage 800 G -> 700 G (full reimburse 800, minus 100)
130	- Steel sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G -> 400 G (50% = 500, minus 100)
131	
132	### E9: Multiple items same type (R17)
133	- Policy covers 2 swords -> insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G
134	- Both swords damaged -> two separate damage entries, each with own deductible
135	- More damage entries than insured items -> reject (non-zero exit)
136	
137	### E10: Cap exhaustion (R12)
138	- Sword + amulet -> insurance sum 1600 G, cap 3200 G
139	- Cursed sword (insurance value 1000 G, premium 165 G) -> cap 2000 G (cap based on unmodified insurance value)
140	- Sword + 3 runes (block) -> insurance sum 1750 G (1000 + 3x250); block doesn't affect insurance sum
141	- Sword insured (sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G); two claims of 1500 G each:
142	  - First claim: payout 1400 G, remaining cap 600 G
143	  - Second claim: payout 600 G (capped), remaining cap 0 G
144	
145	### E11: Rounding (R10)
146	- Premium 197.5 G -> 198 G (rounded up)
147	- Payout 350.5 G -> 350 G (rounded down)
148	
149	### E12: Edge cases (R20)
150	- Empty item list -> premium 5 G (only processing fee)
151	- Unknown item type in quote -> non-zero exit, error to stderr
152	- Damage to item not in policy -> non-zero exit, error to stderr
153	- Negative damage amount -> non-zero exit, error to stderr
154	
155	### E13: Integration -- newcomer with cursed sword (R4, R7, R9, R16)
156	- Customer: 0 years, no previous contract
157	- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3)
158	- Premium: 100 base + 50 curse + 10 first insurance = 160 + 5 fee = 165 G
159	
160	### E14: Integration -- long-standing customer's second contract (R4, R5, R6, R7, R8, R9, R16, Q3)
161	- Customer: 3 years, second quote in scenario
162	- Item: cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
163	- Premium: 100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-enchant - 20 loyalty + 10 first insurance - 15 follow-up = 155 + 5 fee = 160 G
164	- First insurance surcharge applies even for existing customers (every item in a quote is a first insurance)
165	
166	---
167	
168	## Questions / Clarifications
169	
170	### Q1: "Alike" components
171	"Alike" means exactly the same type. 2 runes + 1 moonstone = no block. 3 runes + 3 moonstones = two blocks (60 + 60).
172	
173	### Q2: Modifier scope on multi-item policies
174	Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the individual item's base premium. Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to the policy base premium (sum of all item base premiums after item-specific adjustments).
175	
176	### Q3: "First insurance" meaning
177	Every item in a `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history. The 10% surcharge always applies. The follow-up contract discount is separate and applies to contracts after the customer's first.
178	
179	---
180	
181	## Per-test Rationale
182	
183	Each `it.todo()` entry below maps to a rule/example:
184	
185	### Quote -- base premium tests
186	1. "empty item list -> premium 5 G" -- R9, E12: only processing fee
187	2. "single sword -> premium 115 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 100 base + 10 first-insurance + 5 fee
188	3. "single amulet -> premium 71 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 60 + 6 + 5 = 71
189	4. "single staff -> premium 93 G" -- R1, R7, R9: 80 + 8 + 5 = 93

197	10. "2 runes + 1 moonstone (no block) -> 88 G" -- R3, Q1: 75 base + 7.5 first-ins + 5 = 87.5 -> 88

## Per-test Rationale

Each `it.todo()` in `src/claim-office.spec.ts` maps to spec rules/examples.

**Important arithmetic note for policy-wide modifiers:** Policy-wide modifiers
(loyalty, first-insurance, follow-up) are percentages of the "policy base
premium", which the spec defines as "the sum of all item base premiums"
(the **original** base premiums, not the item-adjusted ones). Item-specific
modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) are added to each item's base premium
separately. The final premium = sum-of-item-adjusted-premiums + policy-wide-
modifier-amounts + 5 G fee.

### Quote -- base premiums (tests 1-5)
1. empty item list -> 5 G -- R9, E12: 0 + 5 fee
2. single sword -> 115 G -- R1, R7, R9: 100 item-adj + 10 first-ins (10% of 100) + 5 fee

4. single staff -> 93 G -- R1, R7, R9: 80 + 8 + 5
